commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the Codex app-server event projector to pass accumulated reasoning text to `onReasoningStream` and adds a regression test for progressive reasoning accumulation.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +23. Total +24 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main accumulates Codex reasoning internally but sends raw deltas, while the Discord progress path formats each callback payload as replacement-style reasoning text.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof from the actual runtime/channel path, not a mechanical code repair.

target.title: fix(codex): send accumulated reasoning text in onReasoningStream
target.body:
## Summary

The Codex event projector sends each reasoning delta chunk directly to `onReasoningStream`, but the Discord channel handler calls `message.edit()` with the received text, replacing the previous content. Each delta overwrites the prior reasoning block instead of accumulating, so Discord users see only the last delta fragment instead of the complete reasoning text.

## Fix

Changed `onReasoningStream` to receive the accumulated reasoning text from `reasoningTextByItem` instead of the raw delta. The accumulation already happens in the Map; the fix passes the accumulated value to the callback so Discord's `message.edit()` shows the complete reasoning.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord reasoning stream shows only the last delta chunk instead of full accumulated reasoning text
Real environment tested: macOS arm64, Node 26, built from patched source checkout
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node -e simulation of the reasoning stream callback behavior before and after the fix
Evidence after fix:
```
=== Simulating Codex reasoning stream fix ===

BEFORE (origin/main) - onReasoningStream receives raw delta:
  onReasoningStream({ text: "Think" })
  onReasoningStream({ text: "ing about " })
  onReasoningStream({ text: "the problem…" })
  -> Discord shows only last chunk: "the problem…"

AFTER (this patch) - onReasoningStream receives accumulated text:
  onReasoningStream({ text: "Think" })
  onReasoningStream({ text: "Thinking about " })
  onReasoningStream({ text: "Thinking about the problem…" })
  -> Discord shows full reasoning: "Thinking about the problem…"
```
Observed result after fix: each onReasoningStream call receives the full accumulated reasoning text so Discord message.edit() replaces the block with the complete content instead of just the latest fragment
What was not tested: Live Discord bot with a model that streams reasoning deltas (requires Discord bot credentials and a reasoning-capable model)

Closes #83983
target.author: sebtardif
